title: WIN-033 defense preemption: dome claims narrow SCP-specific dimming, not gradient
target: WIN-033 detail_verdict_text — dome defender reframes claim as 'SCP specifically dim' not 'smooth gradient for all southern stars.' Our current text lists Canopus/Achernar/Alpha Centauri as counterexamples, but without framing why a gradient prediction is implied. Analyst Mode 3: add one sentence before the post-hoc paragraph: 'A genuine aetheric-depth model entails a smooth declination-dependent extinction gradient — stars at δ=−80° should be more dimmed than stars at δ=−50°, and the SCP region should show a continuous radial fade. Gaia EDR3 photometry of 1.8 billion stars shows no such gradient.' This preempts the dome's retreat to 'only the pole specifically.'
